advice:
Here's your roster so far:

Jahmyr Gibbs (RB, Round 1), Amon-Ra St. Brown (WR, Round 2), Trey McBride (TE, Round 3), RJ Harvey (RB, Round 4), Zay Flowers (WR, Round 5).

You're on the clock at pick 59. Your only unfilled starting slot is QB, plus 2 FLEX and 5 bench spots.

Take Jayden Daniels (QB, 20.03 VORP).

Here's why:

1. You need a QB and this is the right time. Daniels is the top QB on the board at 20.03 VORP, edging Jalen Hurts (19.73) by a hair. Both are elite options, but Daniels has the slight edge right now.

2. QB VORP is about to get thin fast. After Daniels and Hurts, the next QB is Joe Burrow at just 10.88 VORP — a massive cliff. If you wait another round, you risk losing both top options.

3. Your starting core is locked in at RB, WR, and TE. With no positional urgency elsewhere, there's no reason to delay QB any longer.

4. You're on the clock — grab Daniels now and use your remaining picks to build FLEX depth and a handcuff or two.
